title: Outea bijuga
taxonomy: Outea bijuga is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and is a member of the genus Outea, which consists of several species of plants. The Annonaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that are often found in tropical regions. Members of the Annonaceae family are typically characterized by their distinctive flowers and fruits. Within the broader context of plant taxonomy, Outea bijuga is classified as a monocot, a group of flowering plants that have one cotyledon in their seeds.

intro: Outea bijuga, also known as the "Gamboge tree," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia that produces a resinous sap used for medicinal and culinary purposes. The health benefits of Outea bijuga include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Outea bijuga resin include xanthones, flavonoids, and limonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
